data.title: Ramky Infra shares rally after ₹2,085 crore order win, 50% of its market cap
data.description:
Shares of Ramky Infrastructure Ltd. gained as much as 8% on Friday, September 19, after the company secured a ₹2,085 crore Hybrid Annuity Model (HAM) project from the Hyderabad Metropolitan Water Supply and Sewerage Board.

The contract, awarded under the Mallannasagar Water Supply scheme, involves execution of the Godavari Drinking Water Supply Scheme Phase II and Phase III. The project aims to channel Godavari water into the Osmansagar and Himayathsagar reservoirs to rejuvenate the Musi River.

The project entails construction over two years, followed by operations and maintenance for 10 years under the Hybrid Annuity Model.

With this order, Ramky Infrastructure's order book stood at ₹7,650 crore as of June 30, 2025, accounting for about 27% of its total order book.

The smallcap company had a market capitalisation of ₹4,240.44 crore as of Friday.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0327 GMT - Copper rises in early Asian trading, shrugging off a stronger dollar. The three-month LME copper contract is 0.35% higher at $9,020.00 a ton. The bull market in precious metals could broaden and shift into base metals in 2026, supported by the prospect of new dovish Fed leadership, lower U.S. rates and downward pressure on the dollar, Citi analysts say in a report. Risks to copper prices look balanced to Citi as near-term physical demand headwinds offsets a more constructive macro and fundamentals backdrop from 2026. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0309 GMT - Iron ore prices are higher amid solid demand, Baocheng Futures analysts say in a research note. Chinese steel mills are replenishing stocks before the coming holidays, the analysts say. That coincides with a slight rebound in terminal iron-ore consumption, which has also propped up prices, they say. However, the upward momentum could be limited. While overseas shipments are increasing substantially, China's domestic iron ore supply is recovering as well, they note. Iron ore prices will likely fluctuate at high levels in tandem with changes in supply and demand, they add. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 0.4% higher at CNY804.5 a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0302 GMT - Palm oil prices rise in early Asian trade amid bargain hunting. The recent appreciation of the ringgit may have weighed on overall demand for crude palm oil, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. A firmer ringgit tends to pressure crude palm oil prices as the commodity is traded in the Malaysian currency. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency's proposal to focus biofuel blending targets on large refiners dampens demand for vegetable oils, it adds. AmInvestment Bank pegs support for CPO futures at 4,381 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,568 ringgit a ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives December contract is 10 ringgit higher at 4,445 ringgit a ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0023 GMT - Ansell's confidence that it can offset the impact of tariffs helps keep Citi neutral on the stock. Analyst Laura Sutcliffe writes in a note that the personal-protective equipment manufacturer's sales growth will increase as a result of its price rises, but that the impact on Ebit will be neutral. She tells clients that some of the price rises have already been implemented and that the non-discretionary nature of the products supports acceptance by customers. Sutcliffe wouldn't be surprised if Ansell announces a new set of performance goals, but for now sees a multiple of 15 times fiscal 2027 core earnings as fair. Citi raises its target price 6.0% to A$35.50. Shares are up 1.6% at A$33.76. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

2340 GMT - Gold edges lower in the early Asian session amid a cautious mood. The market still sees a high probability of another 25bps rate cut by the Fed in October, but the central bank's guidance leaned slightly hawkish, Sky Links Capital Group's Daniel Takieddine says in an email. Fed officials seem reluctant to fully endorse deeper easing, with the FOMC meeting's outcome leaving the precious metal sensitive to coming economic data, the co-founder and CEO adds. Spot gold is 0.1% lower at $3,638.75/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2334 GMT - Pantoro Gold's stock has more than tripled in value since the start of the year, supported by prices of the precious metal repeatedly hitting new record highs. "Pantoro appears to have turned a corner operationally and we see upside to FY 2026 guidance given recent performance," analyst Ross Bennett says. "However, the recent stock-price rally has somewhat diminished short-term upside." Morgans starts Pantoro at "hold," with a A$5.33/share price target. It expects current commodity prices to help drive solid earnings and production growth for Pantoro. "These are complemented by >50% Ebitda margin in our base case," Morgans adds. Pantoro ended Thursday at A$5.35. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

2018 GMT - Livestock futures trading on the CME close higher — with the most-active live cattle contract closing up 0.6% to $2.324 a pound and lean hog futures finishing up 0.1% to 87.75 cents a pound. Both cattle and hogs have reports from the USDA coming that will shape the direction of those futures contracts, with the Cattle on Feed report due out tomorrow and the quarterly Hogs and Pigs report next Thursday. Analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast that the amount of cattle on feedlots for the month through Sept. 1 would be slightly lower, while marketings of cattle on feedlots may slide by as much as 13%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1921 GMT - Oil futures add to yesterday's losses as the high-demand summer season begins to wind down and the market waits to see what if any additional sanctions are placed on Russian oil. The big decline in U.S. crude stocks reported by the EIA is attributed mostly to the jump in exports. "While the drawdown in crude inventories was impressive, it seems to be mostly export driven with the help of near multi-year lows in the U.S. dollar, and not necessarily from domestic demand," Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial says in a note. WTI settles down 0.7% at $63.57 a barrel, which Kissler sees as the near-term support. Brent falls 0.8% to $67.44 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1844 GMT - Gold futures fall further, with investors having the benefit of Fed Chair Powell's press conference following the central bank's 25 basis point rate cut Wednesday. "I don't think metals liked Powell's comment regarding his hardline stance against the idea of a 50 [basis-point] cut," says John Caruso of RJO Futures in a note. Caruso adds that longer-term, a Fed program with several rate cuts ahead is supportive for gold prices. Front-month gold futures finish down 1% to $3,643.70 a troy ounce, marking the second consecutive day that front-month gold settled lower. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1825 GMT - Grain futures on the CBOT are lower, with traders seen as taking a hands-off approach as harvest season begins in earnest. "Limited news with fund buying absent from the marketplace, allowing corn, soybeans and wheat to drift lower in low volume," says Brian Hoops of Midwest Market Solutions. Hoops adds that there are murmurs of better-than-expected crop yields out of the western Corn Belt, which may be adding pressure to grain prices. An uptick in grain futures seen in mid-August appeared to hit resistance this week. Most-active CBOT corn is down 0.6%, soybeans are down 0.8%, and wheat falls 0.8%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1730 GMT - Crude futures are losing ground for a second straight session as the market turns attention back to supply and demand. "People are talking about too much supply chasing too little demand. There is demand out there, but it has not been growing at the same speed" as supply, Carole Nakhle, CEO of Crystol Energy says in a Welligence Energy Analytics webcast. "But prices have not crashed, which means that we are still able to absorb the additional barrels from OPEC+ and elsewhere." Economic data don't translate into strong demand growth, but China is still buying oil for storage. "It raises the question of how much further they can go accumulating all those barrels and for how long," Nakhle added. WTI is down 0.9% at $63.46 a barrel and Brent is 0.9% lower at $67.35. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1635 GMT - More vessels loaded with grain are leaving the U.S. Gulf, according to data from the USDA. In its latest Grain Transportation Report, the agency says that an average 29 vessels were loaded with grain at a U.S. Gulf port per week over the past four weeks, versus an average of 24 vessels per week in the prior four weeks. Higher demand for U.S. corn exports is one explanation, the USDA says, reflecting what the agency says is "U.S. export competitiveness and robust early-season demand." Low prices per bushel and a weaker U.S. dollar are helping propel demand. Most-active CBOT corn futures are down 0.6%, while soybeans sink 0.7% and wheat falls 0.6%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Malaysia Export Growth Eases to 1.9% in August
data.description:
Exports from Malaysia grew by 1.9% year-on-year to MYR 131.6 billion in August 2025, following a downwardly revised 6.5% increase in the previous month.

This marked the second consecutive month of annual export growth, primarily driven by higher shipments to China (10.4%), Taiwan (32.7%), and the EU (9.7%).

In contrast, exports to the US plunged by 16.7%, weighed down by new US tariffs.

data.description:
** Shares of Wolong Electric Group drop 9.9% to 51.08 yuan, on track for the biggest one-day pct drop since August 21

** Chinese electric motors equipment and storage batteries producer notes of the recent rally of its stock and alerts investors of the risks associated

** Co also says certain media outlets categorised it under robotics-related concepts, but there have been no significant changes in its core business operations, nor major adjustments to the market environment or industry policies

** YTD, stock up 296%, benchmark CSI 300 Index up 14.7%

data.title: BOJ ASSET SELLOFF PLANS: VALUES ARE ANNUAL PACE FOR ALL ASSETS
PROJECTS: Dar Al Arkan discloses land plots in Riyadh facing White Land Tax
data.description:
Staff Writer

Dar Al Arkan, one of the largest listed real estate developers in Saudi Arabia by asset size and market value, said that 2.83 million square metres (sqm) of its land falls under White Land Tax, which is based on its initial assessment of lands within the designated geographical zones.

Nearly 2.65 million sqm of land falls in north Riyadh and is subject to a 5 percent tax, the developer said in a statement to the Saudi stock exchange. Another 181,000 sqm of land falls in north Riyadh within the 10 percent slab.

The initial assessment indicates that all lands, excluding north Riyadh, will maintain the status quo on their applicability, as in earlier years.

Dar Al Arkan will conduct a detailed analysis of each of the land parcels to assess and evaluate the applicability and commercial impact, the statement said.

Earlier this month, Saudi Real Estate Company (Al Akaria), another Saudi-listed developer, said 487,840 sqm of its Riyadh land bank, constituting 4.65 percent of the total 10.48 million sqm land bank, falls within the White Land Tax.

First Avenue for Real Estate Development Company recently launched a new initiative for landowners in Riyadh to transform their idle lands into income-generating assets.

Saudi Ministry of Municipalities and Housing has stated the White Land and Vacant Property Fees Law is aimed at increasing real estate supply, improving the efficiency of underutilised assets and promoting fair competition.

(Editing by Anoop Menon) (anoop.menon@lseg.com)

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Nvidia's Intel Deal Boosts Sentiment
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

U.K. retail sales, public sector finances; France monthly business survey; no major corporate trading updates expected

Opening Call:

European stock futures traded slightly higher early Friday. Asian stock benchmarks were mostly higher; the dollar was flat; Treasury yields were up; while oil futures fell and gold inched higher.

Equities:

Stock futures point to a higher open in Europe on Friday, after Nvidia's deal to invest $5 billion in Intel added fuel to a rally on Wall Street following the Federal Reserve's move Wednesday to cut interest rates

The Nasdaq Composite Index logged its 27th closing high of 2025, with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also hitting new highs. All three indexes were driven by technology shares, the best-performing sector of the S&P. "We're getting a little bit of adjustment on rates and we're seeing this AI train just full speed ahead," said Marta Norton, chief investment strategist at wealth manager Empower.

"The market is landing on that it's not likely a one-off cut," said Michael Antonelli, a managing director at Baird. Interest-rate futures showed that traders see an 80% chance of a half-point rate cut by the end of the year, according to CME Group data, up from 75% Thursday morning.

Forex:

The U.S. economy and financial markets are showing some resilience and potentially U.S. exceptionalism again, Maybank analysts said.

Markets have reassessed the Fed's stance, with traders taking a more positive view of the FOMC's decision and guidance, NAB's Rodrigo Catril said. Fed Chair Powell's "meeting by meeting" guidance is perceived as prudent, the senior FX strategist said.

Bonds:

Treasury yields continued to rise, with investors in the bond market adjusting their positions following the Fed's shift in monetary policy.

Although the Fed's decision to lower its policy rate by a quarter-percentage point was widely expected, there had been some "creeping hope" in the market for a larger "surprise" cut of a half-percentage point, said Russ Brownback, deputy chief investment officer of fixed income at BlackRock.

Also, the Fed's messaging "could have signaled a bit more of a dovish-forward view," considering the labor market has weakened and tariffs don't appear to be creating "broader knock-on effects on inflation," he said.

Energy:

Oil futures fell, as the high-demand summer season begins to wind down and market participants wait to see what, if any, additional sanctions are placed on Russian oil.

The big decline in U.S. crude stocks reported by the EIA is attributed mostly to a jump in exports. "While the drawdown in crude inventories was impressive, it seems to be mostly export driven with the help of near multiyear lows in the U.S. dollar, and not necessarily from domestic demand," Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial said.

Metals:

Gold edged higher in Asia amid a cautious mood. The market still sees a high probability of another 25-basis-point rate cut by the Fed in October, but the central bank's guidance leaned slightly hawkish, Sky Links Capital Group's Daniel Takieddine said.

Fed officials seem reluctant to fully endorse deeper easing, with the FOMC meeting's outcome leaving the precious metal sensitive to coming economic data, the CEO added.

-

Copper rose in Asian trading. The bull market in precious metals could broaden and shift into base metals in 2026, supported by the prospect of new dovish Fed leadership, lower U.S. rates and downward pressure on the dollar, Citi analysts said.

-

Iron ore prices strengthened amid solid demand, Baocheng Futures analysts said. Chinese steel mills are replenishing stocks before the coming holidays, coinciding with a slight rebound in terminal iron-ore consumption that has also propped up prices, they said.

However, the upward momentum could be limited. While overseas shipments are increasing substantially, China's domestic iron ore supply is recovering as well. Iron ore prices will likely fluctuate at high levels in tandem with changes in supply and demand, they added.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Trump's Team Explores Government-Backed Manufacturing Boost

WASHINGTON-President Trump's team is weighing a plan to spur the construction of factories and other infrastructure in a bid to jump-start the American manufacturing sector, according to documents and people familiar with the discussions.

Under the plan, the administration would use money from a $550 billion investment fund established as part of trade negotiations with Japan to invest in the development of semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, critical minerals, energy, ships and quantum computing.

U.K. Consumer Sentiment Falls Back

The mood of British consumers dampened a little this month, with expectations of a slowing economy and the potential for tax increases later this year threatening an even greater downturn.

The headline level of consumer confidence fell back by two points to minus 19 in September, according to research group GfK's monthly index, published with the Nuremberg Institute for Market Decisions on Friday.

Europe's EV Revolution Is on Track. How Auto Makers Plan to Win.

While Donald Trump's U.S. slams the brakes on the electric-vehicle transition, the European Union is keeping its foot to the floor, probably.

An EU statute passed in 2022 will ban the sale of new gasoline-powered internal-combustion passenger cars by 2035.

Protesters in France Push Back on Efforts to Rein In Spending

PARIS-Hundreds of thousands of protesters poured into the streets across France on Thursday, piling pressure on President Emmanuel Macron and his newly appointed prime minister to abandon any austerity measures aimed at repairing the country's depleted public finances.

A day of mostly peaceful protests turned violent as night fell and protesters clashed with police in Place de la Nation in Paris. Protesters hurled projectiles at columns of police. Officers clad in riot gear released tear gas before charging at crowds that refused to disperse.

Jensen Huang Walks an AI Tightrope Between the U.S. and China

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is trying to keep both the U.S. and China happy. It is proving to be a tricky high-wire act.

The chip maker drew praise from President Trump for its $5 billion investment in rival Intel, which recently struck a deal to give a 10% stake to the U.S. government. Huang was beaming on a joint video call with Intel Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan as he discussed a new partnership to make processors for data centers and personal computers.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Friday

06:00/GER: Aug PPI

06:00/UK: Aug Public sector finances

06:00/UK: Aug UK monthly retail sales figures

06:45/FRA: Sep Monthly business survey (goods-producing industries)

07:00/SPN: Jul Industrial Orders & Turnover

08:00/ICE: Aug Harmonized CPI

08:30/UK: 2Q Bank of England statistics on UK banks' external claims

08:30/UK: Aug Monthly Insolvency statistics

15:59/GRE: Jul Balance of Payments

16:59/LUX: 2Q Balance of Payments

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: BOJ REPEATS JULY QUARTERLY OUTLOOK VIEW: JAPAN ECONOMIC GROWTH LIKELY TO MODERATE AMID HIGH UNCERTAINTY OVER TRADE ROWS
Australia's Santos CEO bets on cash flow boost as third suitor walks away
data.description:
Key points:
  • CEO Gallagher says key shareholders want him to stay
  • New production in Australia, Alaska set to boost cash flow
  • Santos is open to selling or selling down assets, CEO says

By Scott Murdoch and Renju Jose

Santos boss Kevin Gallagher said on Friday he has no plans to retire and expects a sharp jump in cash flow in the next few years to boost the Australian gas producer's shares, after its third suitor in seven years scrapped a takeover bid.

An $18.7 billion bid for Santos by a consortium led by Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) collapsed on Wednesday over disagreements on commercial terms, which sparked analyst speculation that CEO Gallagher, in the job since 2016, may face pressure to quit.

Gallagher said cash flow improvements as two new projects in Australia and Alaska start producing should help Santos' share price, which has significantly underperformed the market in the past three years.

"The last three or four years have been tough for Santos. We got smashed in COVID and then of course with the heavy investment period from 2022 through to today and that has suppressed our share price. That's a fact of life," he said.

"But I'm looking forward now to a lower capital expenditure intensity over the next two years, higher cash flows from new production from Barossa and Pikka, supplementing the strong portfolio."

The $4.5 billion Barossa gas project off northwestern Australia and the $3.1 billion Pikka oil project in Alaska are due to start producing within the next six months.

Investment bank Jarden estimates Santos' free cash flow will jump from $293 million in 2025 to $2.448 billion in 2027.

Gallagher said while Santos would evaluate any takeover approach, including asset sales or sell-downs, the company did not need to find a buyer.

"We're not running a sales process for the company. The reason people are looking at Santos is because we've got a good portfolio," Gallagher told reporters on a conference call.

"There's a lot of future value that we want to develop in a disciplined way for our shareholders," he said, highlighting that its assets have development potential close to existing infrastructure.

"That's a to-die-for scenario in an industry these days," Gallagher said.

He said as long as shareholders and the board wanted him in the job, "I'm happy to stay here," adding that key shareholders were asking him to remain focused on the business.

Santos shares were up 0.82% on Friday to A$6.80 after falling by more than 13% on Thursday in the first trading session since ADNOC's overseas unit XRG said it was walking away from its proposed takeover.

XRG baulked after it was revealed capital gains tax payments were due soon on Santos' assets in Papua New Guinea, according to a person familiar with the matter.

data.title: Malaysia's Export Growth Slowed in August
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong

KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia--Growth in Malaysia's exports slowed in August, despite higher shipments of manufactured and agriculture goods.

Exports rose 1.9% from a year earlier to 131.6 billion ringgit, equivalent to $31.36 billion, compared with revised growth of 6.5% for July. Imports fell 5.9% to 115.47 billion ringgit, the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry said Friday.

That resulted in a trade surplus of 16.13 billion ringgit, compared with a 14.61 billion ringgit surplus in July.

The median forecast in a Wall Street Journal poll of six economists had expected exports to rise 3.8% and imports to decline 0.6%, resulting in a trade surplus of 11.68 billion ringgit.

Analysts remain concerned about Malaysia's export outlook, as uncertainties over U.S. semiconductor tariffs and sector-specific trade measures continue to pose downside risks.

President Trump's recent plan to impose "fairly substantial" tariffs on semiconductor imports poses risks for Malaysia, a top global exporter, TA Securities analyst Chan Mun Chun said in a note.

Malaysia exported 60.6 billion ringgit worth of semiconductors to the U.S. in 2024, and higher costs could weigh on both exports and demand, he said. However, the impact may be cushioned, as about 65% of shipments come from U.S. firms operating in Malaysia that could qualify for exemptions.

Given the complexity of supply chains, more exemptions are likely, and relocating production would take decades, he added.

Although the finalized reciprocal U.S. tariff rate was lowered to 19% from the initially proposed 25%, sector-specific tariffs--especially on semiconductors--remain a concern given Malaysia's role as a key exporter to the U.S., Maybank economists Azril Rosli and Suhaimi Ilias said in a note.

As a result, Malaysia's exports may "remain on choppy waters," they said.

Shipments of manufactured goods, which account for 87% of total exports, rose 1.7% from a year earlier, the data showed. Exports of electrical and electronic products, which make up about 42% of exports, increased 10.1%. Agriculture goods exports jumped 4.5%, driven by stronger palm oil and palm oil-based agriculture products shipments amid higher export prices.

On a month-over-month basis, exports fell 6.0% in August, while imports declined 8.0%.

Below are the figures for Malaysia's trade with its five largest export and import trading partners in August:

 
Exports     Value (MYR Millions)   % Change YoY 
Total                  131,597.9            1.9 
Singapore               19,384.0            2.7 
China                   17,236.1           10.4 
USA                     16,367.2          -16.7 
Taiwan                   7,080.1           32.7 
Hong Kong                6,643.1           -5.5 
 
Imports     Value (MYR Millions)   % Change YoY 
Total                  115,472.3           -5.9 
China                   27,501.1            4.0 
Taiwan                  12,813.0           27.1 
Singapore               11,931.8           -9.0 
USA                      9,772.0          -36.7 
South Korea              5,960.0           22.1

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

data.title: Japan 10-Year Yield Jumps After BOJ Decision
data.description:
Japan’s 10-year government bond yield rose above 1.63% on Friday, moving back toward 17-year highs after the Bank of Japan kept its policy rate unchanged at 0.5% for the fifth consecutive meeting, as expected.

The central bank said the economy is recovering moderately but flagged pockets of weakness and cautioned about risks from global trade policies.

It also unanimously agreed to begin selling its ETF and J-REIT holdings.

Meanwhile, data showed Japan’s core inflation rose 2.7% in August, slowing for the third straight month to the lowest since November 2024.

Japanese yields also mirrored US Treasury yield gains after the Federal Reserve delivered a quarter-point cut on Wednesday, projecting two more reductions this year but only one in 2026, countering expectations for multiple cuts next year.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Energy Headlines at 12 AM ET: Exxon Sparks Investor Rights Battle. Why It Could Spread. | Oil ...
data.description:
Exxon Sparks Investor Rights Battle. Why It Could Spread.

The oil giant has unveiled a system to let investors automatically support management proposals. Activists are alarmed.

----

Oil Futures Post Back-to-Back Losses

Oil futures added to yesterday's losses as the high-demand summer season began to wind down and the market waited to see what if any additional sanctions were placed on Russian oil.

----

U.S. Natural Gas Falls on Above-Estimate Storage Build

U.S. natural gas futures fell after the EIA reported an above-consensus 90 billion-cubic-foot increase in underground storage, extending the inventory surplus over the five-year average to more than 200 Bcf.

----

California Wants to Halt Oil Industry Exodus After Years of Climate Focus

Policymakers are trying to stave off a potential fuel-supply crunch while refineries look to close.

----

Exxon Mobil to Halt European Recycling Plant Investments

The company is halting more than $100 million in planned investments for two European chemical recycling projects because of concerns over new rules that the company says will undermine the projects' business case.

----

Santos Confirms XRG Consortium Pulls $18.7 Billion Takeover Bid

Santos confirmed that a consortium led by Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has abandoned a $18.7 billion takeover offer, highlighting disagreements over terms given the length of time it could take for a deal to complete.

----

Mexican Government Issues Debt to Finance Pemex Buybacks

The Mexican government said it issued $13.8 billion in debt in U.S. dollars and euros as part of a series of transactions aimed at capitalizing and shoring up the finances of state oil company Petróleos Mexicanos.

----

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Santos and oil futures in the latest Market Talks covering energy and utilities.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Post Large Decline

Crude oil inventories fell by 9.3 million barrels to 415.4 million barrels in the week ended Sept. 12 as exports almost doubled and imports fell.

----

Beleaguered Offshore Wind Gets Two Small Signs of Hope

The Department of Justice filed a legal brief earlier this month supporting Empire Wind, an offshore wind project near New York's Long Island that the administration had previously halted.

----

Oil, Gas Investment Needed to Keep Output Steady as Field Declines Accelerate, IEA Says

The world must invest an average of$540 billion through 2050 in the upstream sector or face a reduced global production of 5.5 million barrels a day for the next decade, a new report stated.

----

Energy Department Awards BWX $1.5 Billion Contract

A BWX Technologies unit was awarded a National Nuclear Security Administration contract for work on a Domestic Uranium Enrichment Centrifuge Experiment pilot plant.

data.title: Buyout group EQT kicks off sale of Nordic broadband and data centre business, FT reports
data.description:
EQT has launched the sale of its Nordic superfast broadband network and data centre business GlobalConnect, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

EQT which bought a majority holding in GlobalConnect in 2017, is hoping the business could be valued at 8 billion euros ($9.43 billion), the newspaper said.

($1 = 0.8484 euros)

data.title: BOJ ASSET SELLOFF PLANS: IN A WAY SIMILAR TO SELLING OF JAPANESE SHARES BOUGHT FOR PURCHASE OF HELPING STABILIZING FINANCIAL SYSTEM (FINISHED IN JULY)
Super Retail Group extends slide after CEO ouster over disclosure breach
data.description:
** Australia's Super Retail Group down 0.1% at A$16.94

** Extends week's fall to 4.3% so far, set for second consecutive week of losses

** Auto-parts retailer fell as much as 7.7% on Sept 16, after it ousted CEO and MD Anthony Heraghty over relationship with co's former chief HR officer, saying his earlier disclosure was inadequate

** Co named CFO David Burns as the interim CEO on Sept 16

** Shares traded lower last week on ex-dividend trades

** YTD, stock up 13.4%

data.title: Malaysia Imports Fall 5.9% YoY
data.description: Malaysia’s imports shrank 5.9% year-on-year to MYR 115.47 billion in August 2025, marking the first drop since March and swinging from a 0.6% growth in the prior month.

data.title: Malaysia Posts Largest Trade Surplus in 5 Months
data.description:
Malaysia’s trade surplus surged to MYR 16.1 billion in August 2025, up from MYR 6.4 billion in the same month last year.

It was the largest trade surplus since March, as exports rose while imports declined.

Exports increased by 1.9% year-on-year to MYR 131.6 billion, following a downwardly revised 6.5% rise in July.

Meanwhile, imports fell by 5.9% year-on-year to MYR 115.5 billion.

data.title: Adani Group stocks rise after SEBI dismisses some Hindenburg allegations
data.description:
Adani Group stocks rose between 1% and 10% on Friday, a day after India's market regulator dismissed parts of short-seller Hindenburg Research's stock manipulation allegations against billionaire Gautam Adani and his conglomerate.

While the Securities and Exchange Board of India has cleared two charges against Adani group firms, 22 other orders are pending.

Adani Total Gas led gains among the nine entities with its 10% climb. Adani Enterprises , the flagship firm, rose 4.3%.

Adani Power , which climbed 7.4%, was among the top gainers among the group stocks after Morgan Stanley started coverage on the power producer with "overweight" rating.

The SEBI launched its probe in 2023 after U.S.-based Hindenburg accused Adani Group of using tax havens and concealing related-party transactions. The charges, which the conglomerate had denied, wiped $150 billion off the group's market value at the time, though most of its stocks have since rebounded.

"SEBI has reaffirmed what we have always maintained, that the Hindenburg claims were baseless. Transparency and integrity have always defined the Adani Group," Gautam Adani, chairman of Adani Group, said late Thursday on social media platform X.

Adani Ports rose 2%, while Adani Green and Adani Energy Solutions rose nearly 4% each.

data.title: Ethereum Price Squeezes Tight – Watch Out for Sudden Breakout Anytime
data.description:
Ethereum price started a fresh increase above $4,550. ETH is now consolidating and might attempt to clear the $4,640 resistance.

  • Ethereum is now recovering higher above the $4,580 zone.
  • The price is trading above $4,600 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average.
  • There is a short-term contracting triangle forming with resistance at $4,620 on the hourly chart of ETH/USD (data feed via Kraken).
  • The pair could start a fresh increase if it settles above $4,620 and $4,640.

Ethereum Price Eyes Upside Break

Ethereum price formed a base above $4,420 and started a recovery wave, like Bitcoin. ETH price was able to settle above the $4,500 and $4,520 levels.

The price climbed above the $4,550 and $4,600 resistance levels. The bulls pushed the price above the 50% Fib retracement level of the downward wave from the $4,765 swing high to the $4,416 low. However, the bears are active near the $4,640 level.

The price is facing hurdles near the 61.8% Fib retracement level of the downward wave from the $4,765 swing high to the $4,416 low. Ethereum price is now trading above $4,550 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average.

On the upside, the price could face resistance near the $4,640 level. The next key resistance is near the $4,685 level. The first major resistance is near the $4,765 level. A clear move above the $4,765 resistance might send the price toward the $4,840 resistance. An upside break above the $4,840 region might call for more gains in the coming sessions. In the stated case, Ether could rise toward the $4,880 resistance zone or even $4,920 in the near term.

Another Pullback In ETH?

If Ethereum fails to clear the $4,640 resistance, it could start a fresh decline. Initial support on the downside is near the $4,580 level. The first major support sits near the $4,535 zone.

A clear move below the $4,535 support might push the price toward the $4,465 support. Any more losses might send the price toward the $4,420 region in the near term. The next key support sits at $4,350.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for ETH/USD is losing momentum in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI – The RSI for ETH/USD is now above the 50 zone.

Major Support Level – $4,535

Major Resistance Level – $4,640

data.title: XRP Price Stability Signals Opportunity – Is a Fresh Breakout on the Horizon?
data.description:
XRP price started a fresh increase above the $3.050 resistance. The price is now correcting some gains and might find bids near the $3.020 zone.

  • XRP price is moving higher above the $3.00 support zone.
  • The price is now trading above $3.050 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average.
  • There is a bullish trend line forming with support at $3.040 on the hourly chart of the XRP/USD pair (data source from Kraken).
  • The pair could continue to move up if it clears the $3.120 zone.

XRP Price Holds Support

XRP price remained support above $2.950 and started a fresh increase, like Bitcoin and Ethereum. The price climbed above the $3.020 and $3.080 levels.

The bulls even pushed the price above $3.120. A high was formed at $3.138 and the price is now correcting some gains. There was a move below the $3.10 level. The price tested the 50% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $2.9830 swing low to the $3.138 high.

The price is now trading above $3.050 and the 100-hourly Simple Moving Average. Besides, there is a bullish trend line forming with support at $3.040 on the hourly chart of the XRP/USD pair.

If the bulls protect the $3.050 support, the price could attempt another increase. On the upside, the price might face resistance near the $3.10 level. The first major resistance is near the $3.120 level. A clear move above the $3.120 resistance might send the price toward the $3.20 resistance. Any more gains might send the price toward the $3.2320 resistance. The next major hurdle for the bulls might be near $3.250.

More Downside?

If XRP fails to clear the $3.10 resistance zone, it could continue to move down. Initial support on the downside is near the $3.050 level or the 61.8% Fib retracement level of the upward move from the $2.9830 swing low to the $3.138 high. The next major support is near the $3.020 level.

If there is a downside break and a close below the $3.020 level, the price might continue to decline toward $2.980. The next major support sits near the $2.9150 zone, below which the price could gain bearish momentum.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for XRP/USD is now losing pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for XRP/USD is now below the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $3.050 and $3.00.

Major Resistance Levels – $3.10 and $3.120.

data.title: KAI Earnings Set to Soar on Exports, Overseas Production — Market Talk
data.description: Korea Aerospace Industries is expected to post 2025 revenue and operating profit growth of 10.8% and 46.0% respectively, driven by exports growth and a recovery in its aircraft parts business, says Nomura analyst Eon Hwang. Margins from R&D projects in Malaysia and Poland are also improving and should normalize in 2H 2026 when post-R&D production begins, the analyst adds. Hwang expects KAI's 3Q operating profit to slip 2.7% on year but then surge 244% in 4Q given its aircraft delivery schedule and the completion of high-margin projects in Indonesia and Iraq. For 2026-2028, Hwang projects earnings growth should come from a ramp-up of KF-21 fighter jet production and stronger export margins. Nomura maintains a buy rating on KAI's stock with a KRW130,000 target price. Shares were recently at KRW109,100.(jason.chau@wsj.com)
